topic_title: Edge cases — testing what happens at the boundary of expected input
verdict: PASS with 2 soft findings (assessment_reference). All hard-fail checks pass. The reading is well-structured, within word-count budget, faithful to corpus, beginner-accessible, and correctly scoped. Two assessment references (A4, due week 13) appear in Overview (line 5) and Key Takeaways (line 167) — these are LMS metadata in learner prose and should be removed or softened at HITL #3, but do not constitute a hard fail. No scope_creep detected.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar parses cleanly. Exactly 6 required sections in correct order.

length.notes: 1390 words (target 1200–1600). 1390/230 = 6.04 min (target 6–8 min). Both in range.

citations.notes: All 3 resources cited inline and present in References section. No orphans.

forward_references.notes: All cross-topic references point to earlier topics in the sequence (11.4, 11.7, 11.8). No forward references found.

sampled.assertion: Edge case definition — input at or just beyond boundary
def grade(mark) code example with > vs >= bug

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to corpus. No unsupported claims.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined at first use (edge case, equivalence class, boundary value, off-by-one error, test prediction). Terms from prior topics (falsy, loud failure, silent failure, spec-first) are referenced with topic attribution — acceptable. Concrete analogies precede abstractions throughout. No hedging phrases detected above threshold. Sentences are short and active-voice.

formatting.notes: No paragraphs exceed ~4–5 sentences. 5-step prediction process is a numbered list. Worked example uses numbered steps. Definitions use bold-term / plain-meaning shape throughout (edge case, equivalence class, boundary value, off-by-one error, test prediction). Inline examples accompany every new concept introduced in Key Concepts.

scope.notes: Reading teaches edge cases at appropriate beginner depth. pytest and unittest are NOT mentioned in the reading (they appear only in the corpus's Real-World Patterns section which was correctly excluded). All content maps to the topic title. No advanced concepts taught inline.
